symbols.name: AbstractReelBuilderFactory
symbols.detail: [USED] Transitively used by StandardReelBuilderFactory | [UNIQUE] No RAG data available | [OK] Abstract contract only; no implementation logic to evaluate. | [OVER] Abstract class with exactly one concrete subclass and 0 importers (neither runtime nor type-only). ADR-002 (.anatoly/docs/02-Architecture/04-Design-Decisions.md) documents the intent — test doubles, replay, certification — but that benefit only materialises if engine.ts (or tests) depend on the abstract type. With 0 importers the abstract base is dead scaffolding: nothing can be swapped in without also changing the call site. ADR-002 itself flags the negative consequence ('adds one layer of indirection for what is currently a trivial loop'). | [NONE] No test file exists. Abstract class with no runtime behavior beyond interface contract, but the contract itself is untested. | [UNDOCUMENTED] No JSDoc comment. The abstract contract (purpose of the factory, what `reelCount`/`rowCount` mean, expected return structure) is not described. Name alone does not capture the extensibility rationale or the unused `rowCount` caveat.

symbols.name: StandardReelBuilderFactory
symbols.detail: [USED] Runtime-imported by 1 file: src/engine.ts | [UNIQUE] No RAG data available | [OK] Loop correctly iterates 0..reelCount-1 calling spinReel(i); unused _rowCount is intentional per ADR-002 (reel height fixed at 3 inside spinReel). | [ACCEPTABLE] A simple loop delegating to spinReel(i); the class wrapper is slightly heavier than a plain function, but ADR-002 explicitly justifies making grid construction replaceable in engine.ts without modification. One live importer confirms it is actively used. The unused _rowCount parameter is a known consequence acknowledged in the ADR. | [NONE] No test file exists. Used by src/engine.ts, making it a critical production path. buildReels loop logic and spinReel integration are entirely untested. | [UNDOCUMENTED] No JSDoc on the class or its `buildReels` override. Key omissions: why `_rowCount` is ignored (reel height is implicitly fixed by `spinReel`), what the returned `Symbol[][]` shape represents, and that this is the RNG-backed production implementation.

actions.description: Add JSDoc documentation for exported symbol: `AbstractReelBuilderFactory`
Add JSDoc documentation for exported symbol: `StandardReelBuilderFactory`

file_level.general_notes: Best practices score: 9/10 (0 FAIL, 2 WARN, 15 PASS)

rules.detail: No implicit-any, no type-unsafe patterns in code. tsconfig not visible but the file is compatible with strict mode.
No explicit or implicit `any`. All types are concrete (`Symbol[][]`, `number`).
No union types used; not applicable to this factory file.
No utility-type opportunities missed; the signatures are direct and correct.
Abstract method and override both return mutable `Symbol[][]`. Callers receive a grid they can mutate. Prefer `ReadonlyArray<ReadonlyArray<Symbol>>` to signal the grid is consumed, not modified.
Class-based hierarchy is the consistent convention for factories; no interface/type mixing.
Unused parameter correctly prefixed with `_rowCount`. No other lint violations visible.

Both exported classes (`AbstractReelBuilderFactory`, `StandardReelBuilderFactory`) lack JSDoc. The rationale and contract exist in `.anatoly/docs/02-Architecture/04-Design-Decisions.md` but are not surfaced at the call site.
`import type` used correctly for the type-only `Symbol` import. No deprecated APIs.
Two imports: type-only first, then value import. Both are internal siblings in `src/`. No circular dependency risk and no side-effect imports.
No async operations. `spinReel` is synchronous. No promise handling needed.

No hardcoded secrets, no `eval`, no command injection. Domain: regulated slot machine. RNG certification concerns belong to `reels.ts`/`rng.ts` (where `spinReel` is defined), not to this factory layer which only delegates construction.
Loop is O(reelCount) — trivially bounded (five reels per ADR-002). No N+1 or unnecessary allocations.
Abstract factory is the canonical DI pattern for swapping the grid source. ADR-002 explicitly lists test-double factories as a design goal.
No missed `satisfies`, const type params, or `using` opportunities. The class hierarchy and `import type` are idiomatic modern TypeScript.

Abstract factory is the correct pattern for this utility layer. `_rowCount` is documented in ADR-002 as a known unused parameter (reel height is fixed at 3 by `spinReel`). No React, no HTTP, no event handling concerns apply.

suggestions.description: Return readonly grid to prevent callers from mutating the constructed reel state
suggestions.before: abstract buildReels(reelCount: number, rowCount: number): Symbol[][];
suggestions.after: abstract buildReels(reelCount: number, rowCount: number): ReadonlyArray<ReadonlyArray<Symbol>>;

suggestions.description: Add JSDoc to both exported classes so the factory contract is visible at the call site without consulting the ADR
